verses.sanskrit: नारदस्य तु तद्वाक्यम् श्रुत्वा वाक्य विशारदः ।पूजयामास धर्मात्मा सह शिष्यो महामुनिः ॥१-२-१॥
verses.text:
Then, the great sage Valmiki, a skilled orator and a virtue-minded one, having heard those words of Narada, 
		worshipped him along with disciples. [1-2-1]

verses.sanskrit: यथावत् पूजितः तेन देवर्षिः नारदः तथा ।आपृच्छैव अभ्यनुज्ञातः स जगाम विहायसम् ॥१-२-२॥
verses.text:
That divine sage Narada being worshipped befittingly by Valmiki in that way, on seeking 
permission of Valmiki to leave and having been permitted by Valmiki, went away heaven-ward. 
[1-2-2]

verses.sanskrit: स मुहूर्तं गते तस्मिन् देवलोकम् मुनिः तदा ।जगाम तमसा तीरम् जाह्नव्यात् अविदूरतः ॥१-२-३॥
verses.text: Then, on Narada's going to the world of gods in a trice, that sage Valmiki went to the banks of Tamasa river not far from Jahnavi river. [1-2-3]

verses.sanskrit: स तु तीरम् समासाद्य तमसाया मुनिः तदा ।शिष्यम् आह स्थितम् पार्श्वे दृष्ट्वा तीर्थम् अकर्दमम् ॥१-२-४॥
verses.text: Then that sage Valmiki on reaching the shore of the clean Tamasa, and on beholding a ford of that river, spoke to his disciple who was at his side. [1-2-4]

verses.sanskrit: अकर्दमम् इदम् तीर्थम् भरद्वाज निशामय ।रमणीयम् प्रसन्न अम्बु सन् मनुष्य मनो यथा ॥१-२-५॥
verses.text: "Oh! Bharadwaja, behold this ford which is without dirt, with clear waters and pleasant like a virtuous man's mind. [1-2-5]

verses.sanskrit: न्यस्यताम् कलशः तात दीयताम् वल्कलम् मम ।इदम् एव अवगाहिष्ये तमसा तीर्थम् उत्तमम् ॥१-२-६॥
verses.text:
"My dear! Let the hand-vessel [kamaNDalu] be kept [there]. Let my bark-garment be given to me. I will enter this best 
ford of Tamasa only... [1-2-6]

verses.sanskrit: एवम् उक्तो भरद्वाजो वाल्मीकेन महात्मना ।प्रयच्छत मुनेः तस्य वल्कलम् नियतः गुरोः ॥१-२-७॥
verses.text: Thus being said by the great-souled Valmiki, Bharadwaaja who was obedient to his Guru, gave the bark-garment to the sage [1-2-7]

verses.sanskrit: स शिष्य हस्तात् आदाय वल्कलम् नियतेन्द्रियः ।विचचार ह पश्यन् तत् सर्वतो विपुलम् वनम् ॥१-२-८॥
verses.text:
That self-controlled Sage Valmiki on taking bark-garment from his disciple's hands, and looking in all directions, indeed moved away from that
wide expanse of forest. [1-2-8]

verses.sanskrit: तस्य अभ्याशे तु मिथुनम् चरन्तम् अनपायिनम् ।ददर्श भगवान् तत्र क्रौङ्चयोः चारु निस्वनम् ॥१-२-९॥
verses.text:
There holy sage Valmiki saw a couple of curlews, in the vicinity of that river's 
foreshore, flying there about in togetherness and cooing charmingly. [1-2-9]

verses.sanskrit: तस्मात् तु मिथुनात् एकम् पुमांसम् पाप निश्चयः ।जघान वैरनिलयो निषादः तस्य पश्यतः ॥१-२-१०॥
verses.text:
A tribal hunter with evil intent, and a receptacle of enimity, killed the male 
one among the couple of birds, while Valmiki was looking on. [1-2-10]

verses.sanskrit: तम् शोणित परीताङ्गम् चेष्टमानम् महीतले ।भार्या तु निहतम् दृष्ट्वा रुराव करुणाम् गिरम् ॥१-२-११॥
verses.text:
The wife was moving together with husband with a red crest and with joy, with good wings, always being united with that male bird. On seeing
that bird slain on ground reeling with blood covered wings really made wailing sounds with piteous utterances on being separated from her 
husband. 
[1-2-11, 12]

verses.sanskrit: वियुक्ता पतिना तेन द्विजेन सहचारिणा ।ताम्र शीर्षेण मत्तेन पत्रिणा सहितेन वै ॥१-२-१२॥
तथा विधिम् द्विजम् दृष्ट्वा निषादेन निपातितम् ।ऋशेः धर्मात्मानः तस्य कारुण्यम् समपद्यत ॥१-२-१३॥
verses.text:
On seeing that bird felled that way by the tribal hunter, compassion occassioned in that 
saintly sage Valmiki. [1-2-13]

verses.sanskrit: ततः करुण वेदित्वात् अधर्मो अयम् इति द्विजः ।निशाम्य रुदतीम् क्रौन्चीम् इदम् वचनम् अब्रैइत् ॥१-२-१४॥
verses.text:
Then on seeing the wailing female krounchi bird, out of state of compassion and apperceiving 
the killing of male bird as unjust, the sage uttered this sentence... [1-2-14]

verses.sanskrit: मा निषाद प्रतिष्ठाम्त्व ।मगमः शाश्वतीः समाः ।यत् क्रौङ्च मिथुनात् एक ।मवधीः काम मोहितम् ॥१-२-१५॥
verses.text:
"Oh! violent Hunter, by which reason you have killed one bird of the couple, when it 
was infatuated by passion, for that reason you will never get a state of rest for everlasting years to come..." 
[1-2-15]

verses.sanskrit: तस्य एवम् ब्रुवतः चिन्ता बभूव हृदि वीक्षतः ।शोकार्तेन अस्य शकुनेः किम् इदम् व्याहृतम् मया ॥१-२-१६॥
verses.text: Thus speaking and pondering in his heart, he became thoughtful: "What is this utterance by me in grief and anguish for this bird?". [1-2-16]

verses.sanskrit: चिन्तयन् स महाप्राज्ञः चकार मतिमान् मतिम् ।शिश्यम् च एव अब्रवीत् वाक्यम् इदम् स मुनिपुङ्गवः ॥१-२-१७॥
verses.text:
That great wise one who was an intellectual and the best among sages, on cogitating, made up his mind and also spoke these words to the 
disciple... [1-2-17]

verses.sanskrit: पाद बद्धः अक्षर समः तन्त्री लय समन्वितः ।शोकार्तस्य प्रवृत्तो मे श्लोको भवतु न अन्यथा ॥१-२-१८॥
verses.text:
"While I was tormented by grief, there arose a sentence consisting of four verses possessing metrically posited letters
suitable to be played as song and string music and that will be called Shloka, not otherwise..." [1-2-18]

verses.sanskrit: शिष्यः तु तस्य ब्रुवतो मुनेर् वाक्यम् अनुत्तमम् ।प्रति जग्राह संतुष्टः तस्य तुष्टोअः अभवत् मुनिः ॥१-२-१९॥
verses.text:
Even the disciple happily received what that is articulated by the saint, a unique articulation, 
by which the saint too, was delighted. [1-2-19]

verses.sanskrit: सोऽभिषेकम् ततः कृत्वा तीर्थे तस्मिन् यथाविधि ।तम् एव चिन्तयन् अर्थम् उपावर्तत वै मुनिः ॥१-२-२०॥
verses.text:
Then that saint on performing his bathing in that ford according to custom, and still thinking 
on the purport of his utterance, he returned towards his hermitage. [1-2-20]

verses.sanskrit: भरद्वाजः ततः शिष्यो विनीतः श्रुतवान् गुरोः ।कलशम् पूर्णमादाय पृष्ठतः अनुजगाम ह ॥१-२-२१॥
verses.text:
Then Bharadwaja, the obedient disciple and an erudite scholar, for he heard and learnt many 
scriptures by listening, on taking handy-vessel full with water followed at the behind of 
his mentor. [1-2-21]

verses.sanskrit: स प्रविश्य आश्रम पदम् शिष्येण सह धर्मवित् ।उपविष्टः कथाः च अन्याः चकार ध्यानमास्थितः ॥१-२-२२॥
verses.text:
He that knower of dharma, Valmiki, having entered the threshold of hermitage along with disciples, 
and having seated spoke about the day-to-day teachings and also other things, but he himself 
is preoccupied in cogitation on the verse. [1-2-22]

verses.sanskrit: आजगाम ततः ब्रह्मो लोककर्ता स्वयम् प्रभुः ।चतुर् मुखो महातेजा द्रष्टुम् तम् मुनिपुङ्गवम् ॥१-२-२३॥
verses.text:
Then, the great resplendent Four-faced creator of fourteen worlds, almighty Brahma, arrived 
there on his own, to see that eminent saint Valmiki. [1-2-23]

verses.sanskrit: वाल्मीकिः अथ तम् दृष्ट्वा सहसा उत्थाय वाग्यतः ।प्रान्जलिः प्रयतो भूत्वा तस्थौ परम विस्मितः ॥१-२-२४॥
verses.text:
Then that pious saint Valmiki is highly surprised on seeing Brahma, and on quickly getting 
up from his seat with his palms adjoined humbly, he stood aside, as he is spellbind. [1-2-24]

verses.sanskrit: पूजयामास तम् देवम् पाद्य अर्घ्य आसन वन्दनैः ।प्रणम्य विधिवत् च एनम् पृष्ट्वा च एव निरामयम् ॥१-२-२५॥
verses.text:
Valmiki uninterruptedly venerated Brahma, on inquiring into his well-being, washed his feet, drenched his 
thirst, seated him to rest, and adored at best with customarily obeisance. [1-2-25]

verses.sanskrit: अथ उपविश्य भगवान् आसने परम अर्चिते ।वाल्मीकये च ऋषये सन्दिदेश आसनम् ततः ॥१-२-२६॥
verses.text:
Then god Brahma, who is seated on a high seat, very highly worshipped by Valmiki, also beckoned 
at Valmiki to take a seat. [1-2-26]

verses.sanskrit: ब्रह्मणा समनुज्ञातः सोऽपि उपाविशत् आसने ।उपविष्टे तदा तस्मिन् साक्षात् लोक पितामहे ॥१-२-२७॥
तत् गतेन एव मनसा वाल्मीकिः ध्यानम् आस्थितः।पापात्मना कृतम् कष्टम् वैर ग्रहण बुद्धिना ॥१-२-२८॥
यत् तादृशम् चारुरवम् क्रौन्चम् हन्यात् अकारणात्।शोचन् एव पुनः क्रौन्चीम् उप श्लोकम् इमम् जगौ ॥१-२-२९॥
पुनर् अन्तर्गत मना भूत्वा शोक परायणः।तम् उवाच ततो ब्रह्मा प्रहसन् मुनिपुंगवम् ॥१-२-३०॥
verses.text:
Then, Brahma smilingly spoke to that eminent saint Valmiki, "But, what that is composed is 
a verse only... and there is no need to think through... [1-2-30]

verses.sanskrit: श्लोक एवास्त्वयाम् बद्धो न अत्र कार्या विचारणा।मत् च्छन्दात् एव ते ब्रह्मन् प्रवृत्ते अयम् सरस्वती ॥१-२-३१॥
verses.text:
"Oh, Brahman, that speech of yours sprang forth at my wish alone, hence oh, eminent sage, 
you shall render the legend of Rama, in its entirety... [1-2-31]

verses.sanskrit: रामस्य चरितम् कृत्स्नम् कुरु त्वम् ऋषिसत्तम ।धर्मात्मनो भगवतो लोके रामस्य धीमतः ॥१-२-३२॥
verses.text:
"You shall narrate the legend of Rama, the virtuous, intellectual and an intrepid one, and 
a godlike person in this world as well, as you have heard it from sage Narada. [1-2-32]

verses.sanskrit: वृत्तम् कथय धीरस्य यथा ते नारदात् श्रुतम् ।रहस्यम् च प्रकाशम् च यद् वृत्तम् तस्य धीमतः ॥१-२-३३॥
verses.text:
"The adventures of valorous Rama along with Lakshmana, and the misadventures of demons, known 
or unknown in every detail, and even the plight of Vaidehi which is either revealed or un-revealed 
so far, and whatever legend that has happened, all that will also be known to you, even if 
it were to be unknown, as yet... [1-2-33-34]

verses.sanskrit: रामस्य सह सौमित्रे राक्षसानाम् च सर्वशः ।वैदेह्याः च एव यद् वृत्तम् प्रकाशम् यदि वा रहः ॥१-२-३४॥
तत् च अपि अविदितम् सर्वम् विदितम् ते भविष्यति ।न ते वाक् अनृता काव्ये काचित् अत्र भविष्यति ॥१-२-३५॥
verses.text:
"You shall versify the heart pleasing and merit-yielding legend of Rama, and not a single 
word of yours will not be false in this epic... [1-2-35]

verses.sanskrit: कुरु राम कथाम् पुण्याम् श्लोक बद्धाम् मनोरमाम् ।यावत् स्थास्यन्ति गिरयः सरितः च महीतले ॥१-२-३६॥
verses.text:
"As long as the mountains and even rivers flourish on the surface of the earth, so long the 
legend of Ramayana will flourish in this world... [1-2-36]

verses.sanskrit: तावत् रामायण कथा लोकेषु प्रचरिष्यति ।यावत् रामस्य च कथा त्वत् कृता प्रचरिष्यति ॥१-२-३७॥
तावत् ऊर्ध्वम् अधः च त्वम् मत् लोकेषु निवत्स्यसि ।इति उक्त्वा भगवान् ब्रह्मा तत्र एव अन्तरधीयत ।ततः स शिष्यो भगवान् मुनिः विस्मयम् आययौ ॥१-२-३८॥
verses.text:
On saying thus that Divinity Brahma vanished then and there only, and then that godly sage 
Valmiki came by astonishment, along with his disciples. [1-2-38]

verses.sanskrit: तस्य शिष्याः ततः सर्वे जगुः श्लोकम् इमम् पुनः ।मुहुर् मुहुः प्रीयमाणाः प्राहुः च भृश विस्मिताः ॥१-२-३९॥
verses.text:
Then all of the disciples of Valmiki sang this verse time and again very delightedly, and 
much astonished they also recited this verse, reciprocally. [1-2-39]

verses.sanskrit: समाक्षरैः चतुर्भिः यः पादैः गीतो महर्षिणा ।सः अनुव्याहरणात् भूयः शोकः श्लोकत्वम् आगतः ॥१-२-४०॥
verses.text:
Equally lettered, four-footed is that verse when great Sage Valmiki articulated it, and when 
repetitively recited by one and all, it attained prominence as verse proper. [1-2-40]

verses.sanskrit: तस्य बुद्धिः इयम् जाता महर्षेः भावितात्मनः ।कृत्स्नम् रामायणम् काव्यम् ईदृशैः करवाण्यहम् ॥१-२-४१॥
verses.text:
Born is an intuition in that great sage and contemplated soul asserting that "I will compose 
entire Ramayana, the epic, in suchlike verses...' [1-2-41]

verses.sanskrit: उदार वृत्त अर्थ पदैः मनोरमैःतदा अस्य रामस्य चकार कीर्तिमान् ।सम अक्षरैः श्लोक शतैः यशस्विनोयशस्करम् काव्यम् उदार दर्शनः ॥१-२-४२॥
verses.text:
That celebrated sage and magnanimous seer Valmiki then authored the highly renowned Rama's 
legend extolling Rama's renown, with symmetrically worded verses, and words versified to yield 
meanings semantically, prosody free-flowing, and with hundreds of such verses. [1-2-42]

verses.sanskrit: तद् उपगत समास सन्धि योगम्सम मधुरोपनत अर्थ वाक्य बद्धम् ।रघुवर चरितम् मुनिप्रणीतम्दश शिरसः च वधम् निशामय अध्वम् ॥१-२-४३॥
verses.text:
That epic contains uncomplicated compounds, conjunctions and conjugations, and has expressive 
sentences that are well knit and led evenly and sweetly, and that legend pertains to the best one 
from Raghu's dynasty, namely Rama, which also includes the extermination of the Ten headed evil 
named Ravana, and further, it is said by the sage, hence this may now be listened…  [1-2-43]
